source.definition: to put items into a container such as a bag or box for storage or transport

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "pack" comes from the Old French word "pacquet", meaning a bundle or package, which itself derives from the Late Latin "paccus". It refers to the action of placing things together tightly for carrying or storing.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „pack” provine din franceza veche „pacquet”, care înseamnă un pachet sau o legătură, derivat din latina târzie „paccus”. Se referă la acțiunea de a pune lucruri împreună strâns pentru a le transporta sau depozita.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used in contexts such as travel or moving to indicate placing items into bags or suitcases.

target.definition: a pune ceva într-un pachet sau într-o geantă

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb "a împacheta" is borrowed from the French "emballer", meaning "to pack" or "to wrap". It denotes the action of wrapping or placing objects into packaging.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a împacheta” provine din franceză „emballer” („a împacheta”, „a ambala”). Se referă la acțiunea de a înveli sau a pune obiecte într-un ambalaj.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). Frequently used in connection with packing food, gifts, or travel items.

explanation.sourceText: Often used to describe wrapping gifts festively.
explanation.targetText: Folosit adesea pentru împachetarea cadourilor festiv.

explanation.sourceText: Also used for packing food items.

examples.sourceText: I would pack it if I had time.
examples.targetText: Aș împacheta, dacă aș avea timp.

learningTips.text: Remember the English word "pack" as in "pack a suitcase" to connect it with the Romanian "a împacheta".
The Romanian verb ends with "-a", typical for first conjugation verbs, making it easier to conjugate.
Think of "a împacheta" as the opposite of "a despacheta" (to unpack), so you learn two related verbs together.
Associate "impacheta" with the English word "impact" to remember the idea of putting things tightly together.
